9	# Solution Design: Config-Driven Runtime Options
10	
11	## Purpose
12	
13	Eliminate the manual command-layer wiring pattern that copies config
14	fields into options structs at every dispatch site. Replace it with a
15	type-system-enforced contract where every worker, runner, and review
16	path receives a single resolved configuration object that the type
17	system requires. Make the bug class — "config field exists but no
18	caller wires it through, so the loop silently uses defaults" — locally
19	unreachable.
20	
21	## Scope
22	
23	In scope:
24	
25	- Architectural pattern: `ResolvedConfig` (immutable) + per-family
26	  `*Runtime` structs replace the `*Options` family today.
27	- Three production dispatch sites that must call `config.Load`:
28	  CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`, GraphQL `StartWorker`.
29	- Five options families that get migrated in stages:
30	  `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`, `RunOptions`, `ExecuteBeadOptions`,
31	  `CompareOptions`, `QuorumOptions`.
32	- Three latent dead-config-field cases that surface during the
33	  refactor: `NoProgressCooldown`, `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, and
34	  `HeartbeatInterval` are hardcoded constants today; `ContextBudget`
35	  has no config home; `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` is parsed but never
36	  consumed.
37	- Lint enforcement (Stage 4) that mechanically forbids the old
38	  `*Options`-with-durable-fields pattern.
39	- Test coverage uplift: the four disabled config tests
40	  (`*.disabled`) get evaluated and either restored against the new
41	  API or deleted with rationale.
42	
43	Out of scope:
44	
45	- The CONTRACT-003 boundary with `~/Projects/agent`. Investigation
46	  confirmed the agent module does not import `ddx/internal/config`
47	  and ddx pre-resolves at `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:104`
48	  and `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:80`. No agent-repo changes
49	  are required and no contract document needs revision. Agent
50	  v0.9.9 (ADR-005 smart routing) further simplifies the boundary by
51	  removing `ServiceExecuteRequest.PreResolved` — `ResolveRoute` is
52	  now informational and `Execute` re-resolves on its own inputs.
53	  The ddx side just populates `EstimatedPromptTokens` and
54	  `RequiresTools` (new per-invocation hints) on `ServiceExecuteRequest`;
55	  routing decisions are owned upstream.
56	- Routing-layer cleanup driven by agent v0.9.9 (rendering composed
57	  inventory in `ddx agent route-status`, retiring `agent.routing.model_overrides`,
58	  dropping the "No model routes configured" fatal error path).
59	  Tracked separately as a small follow-up batch on top of FEAT-006;
60	  does not block this refactor and is not delivered by it.
61	- The wire shape of `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` /
62	  `agentlib.ExecuteResponse`. The refactor changes how ddx-side state
63	  is organized before the request is built; the request itself is
64	  unchanged.
65	- New CLI commands or operator workflows. This is a structural
66	  refactor; user-visible behavior is preserved.
67	
68	## Problem
69	
70	A reviewer recently caught that FEAT-022 Stage G shipped a
71	`ReviewMaxRetries` field on `*config.Config` plus a resolver, and an
72	`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.ReviewMaxRetries` field that the loop honors,
73	but no caller in `cli/cmd/` ever read the resolved value and assigned
74	it to the options struct. The config field was dead in production. The
75	test that claimed to verify the loop "respects" the override actually
76	injected the field directly into options.
77	
78	Investigation found this is systemic, not a one-off:
79	
80	- 6 production sites that construct `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`,
81	  `RunOptions`, or `ExecuteBeadOptions` across CLI and server.
82	  **Zero** of them call `config.Load` before constructing the options
83	  struct.
84	- Three dispatch entry points (CLI `ddx work` at
85	  `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1997`, server `runWorker` at
86	  `cli/internal/server/workers.go:878`, GraphQL `StartWorker` at
87	  `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:77`) all skip
88	  config entirely and populate options from CLI flags or request
89	  fields only. Every config-derived knob across every options struct
90	  is dead in the same way `ReviewMaxRetries` was.
91	- Two more `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` fields, `NoProgressCooldown` and
92	  `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, are hardcoded constants in
93	  `execute_bead_loop.go:292` with no config field at all. They have
94	  the same shape as `ReviewMaxRetries` would have had if no resolver
95	  existed.
96	- The Go type system permits constructing any `*Options` struct with
97	  zero-value defaults. The compiler cannot tell the implementer
98	  "you forgot to wire this field." Every new config knob is a new
99	  opportunity for the same bug.
100	
101	The pattern accumulated weeks of cruft: each new config knob added a
102	field to `*Options`, a resolver to `*Config`, and silently relied on
103	every caller remembering to bridge them. The reviewer caught one
104	instance because Stage G's acceptance criteria explicitly demanded an
105	end-to-end YAML→loop test. Without that explicit gate, the gap stays
106	invisible.
107	
108	## Approach
109	
110	Three structural changes that compose:
111	
112	### 1. `ResolvedConfig` is the only valid input.
113	
114	A new immutable value type `config.ResolvedConfig` exposes accessors
115	for every durable knob a worker, runner, or review path consumes. The
116	type has no exported fields; clients call methods.
117	
118	A naive "unexported fields" implementation does not actually prevent
119	construction — Go's zero-value rule means
120	`var rcfg config.ResolvedConfig` and `config.ResolvedConfig{}` are
121	both valid expressions in any package. The design closes this escape
122	hatch with a sealed-construction pattern: `ResolvedConfig` carries a
123	single unexported sentinel field set only by `Resolve`. Every public
124	accessor checks the sentinel on the first read and panics with a
125	specific message naming `LoadAndResolve` if the sentinel is unset.
126	A zero-value `ResolvedConfig` thus passes the type check but fails
127	loudly on any access — making the bypass unreachable in any code
128	path that actually consumes the value.
129	
130	`ResolvedConfig` is shared across goroutines without synchronization
131	because no method mutates it. There is no `Set*` API, no public
132	mutator, no JSON unmarshal target.
133	
134	### 2. Runtime structs hold runtime plumbing only.
135	
136	`ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` becomes `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, with all
137	durable fields removed. The same applies to the four other options
138	families. A runtime struct holds:
139	
140	- non-serializable plumbing (`Log io.Writer`, `EventSink`,
141	  `ProgressCh`, `PreClaimHook`)
142	- per-invocation runtime intent (`Once`, `LabelFilter`, `SessionID`,
143	  `WorkerID`)
144	
145	Anything that could plausibly live in config — anything someone might
146	want to set persistently for a project — is forbidden from the runtime
147	struct. The compiler enforces this because the durable fields are
148	removed entirely.
149	
150	Run, RunCompare, RunQuorum, ExecuteBead, and ReviewBead all change
151	signature to:
152	
153	```go
154	func (...) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime XRuntime) (...)
155	```
156	
157	### 3. All three dispatch sites resolve once, hand off everywhere — atomically.
158	
159	The three dispatch entry points (CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`,
160	GraphQL `StartWorker`) all gain a single shared resolution helper:
161	
162	```go
163	rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
164	```
165	
166	`overrides` carries CLI flag values or request fields. The helper
167	loads the project's `.ddx/config.yaml`, applies overrides as a layer,
168	and returns the immutable `ResolvedConfig`. Every downstream call
169	takes that single value.
170	
171	**All three sites must be migrated together for the bug class to
172	actually close.** Migrating only the CLI site leaves `ddx work` from
173	a server context still bypassing config (which is what production
174	actually uses). Migrating only the server site leaves direct CLI
175	runs broken. The migration discipline (see §Migration) requires that
176	the loop migration be considered "complete" only when all three
177	dispatch paths route through `LoadAndResolve`.
178	
179	There is no other supported way to obtain a `ResolvedConfig` in
180	production code. Adding a fourth dispatch entry (e.g. a future REST
181	endpoint that starts a worker) requires calling `LoadAndResolve` at
182	that site.
183	
184	## Why this approach
185	
186	Three alternatives considered and rejected:
187	
188	**Alternative A: builder pattern on options struct.**
189	`agent.NewExecuteBeadLoopOptions(cfg).WithProject(p)`.
190	Surface looks cleaner but does not eliminate the bug class — the
191	builder still has setters per durable field, and a new config field
192	still requires updating the builder. Test code can still call
193	`builder.WithReviewMaxRetries(0)` to bypass real config. Does not
194	make the bypass impossible.
195	
196	**Alternative B: documented immutability of `*Config` shared across
197	callers.** Cheaper to implement (no new type) but defeated by Go's
198	shallow-copy semantics on structs containing maps and pointers.
199	`*Config` carries `PersonaBindings map`, `*AgentConfig` (with
200	`Models map`), `*RoutingConfig` (multiple maps), and
201	`*EvidenceCapsConfig` (`PerHarness map`). A shallow copy aliases all
202	these maps. A caller that runs `cfg2 := *cfg; cfg2.AgentConfig.Models[k] = v`
203	silently mutates the original. Documentation is not enforcement.
204	The type system has to make the failure mode unreachable.
205	
206	**Alternative C: pass `*Config` directly with the existing `*Options`
207	shape preserved.** Would require every caller to read `cfg.Resolve*`
208	methods and copy results into options. This is what we have today.
209	The bug class persists; we only delay it.
210	
211	The chosen approach makes three things simultaneously true:
212	
213	1. The compiler refuses to call `Run` without a `ResolvedConfig`.
214	2. The runtime struct cannot carry a durable field, so a future config
215	   knob has nowhere to bypass to.
216	3. There is exactly one production path that produces a
217	   `ResolvedConfig`, so adding a new config knob requires updating
218	   exactly one place (the `Resolve` method) for it to be visible to
219	   every consumer.
220	
221	## Migration discipline
222	
223	The refactor delivers across many small beads, not a few big stages.
224	Two non-negotiable principles govern every bead:
225	
226	**Green tree.** Every bead leaves the tree fully functional. No bead
227	breaks user-visible behavior — `ddx work` keeps draining the queue,
228	`ddx agent run` keeps dispatching, the server keeps accepting GraphQL
229	mutations. The migration adds new types and call paths *alongside*
230	the existing ones, then retires the old ones in dedicated cleanup
231	beads only after every caller has moved.
232	
233	**Per-bead size budget.** Each bead must fit in a single execute-bead
234	worker window: target <500 LoC of net change, <20 minutes of agent
235	time, under $10 of model spend. Empirically, FEAT-022's beads averaged
236	7-15 minutes and $3-7. Anything that wants to "do the whole loop
237	migration in one shot" is sized wrong and must be split.
238	
239	These principles imply a specific implementation pattern at every
240	seam:
241	
242	1. **Add new alongside old.** Introduce `ResolvedConfig`, `Resolve`,
243	   `LoadAndResolve`, the `*Runtime` structs, and the new `Run`
244	   signatures *alongside* the existing `*Options` types and `Run`
245	   methods. Old methods stay alive and call into the new path
246	   internally if needed; production callers haven't moved yet.
247	2. **Migrate one production caller per bead.** Each dispatch site
248	   moves in its own bead, with a behavioral test that proves config
249	   actually drives the resulting behavior at that site.
250	3. **Migrate test files in groups.** Test sites are mechanical;
251	   group them by file (one test file per bead). Tests on the old
252	   `*Options` API stay green via a temporary shim until the file is
253	   migrated.
254	4. **Retire old types in dedicated cleanup beads.** When and only
255	   when every production and test caller has moved, a final bead
256	   removes the old type and shim.
257	
258	## Stage groupings
259	
260	The bead inventory is structured into stage groupings. Each grouping
261	is a logical milestone, not a single bead. The actual bead count
262	exceeds 25 — see TD-024 §Bead Inventory for the per-bead breakdown.
263	
264	Stage 1 unblocks FEAT-022 Stage G. Stages 2–4 land sequentially or
265	with overlap.
266	
267	**Stage 1 — Foundation + ExecuteBeadLoop.**
268	Introduces `ResolvedConfig`, `Resolve`, `CLIOverrides`,
269	`LoadAndResolve`, the test-config constructors, and the
270	`ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` shape. Adds the new `*Runtime` types and
271	new `RunWithConfig` methods *alongside* existing `*Options` types
272	and `Run` methods (green-tree principle). Adds resolvers for
273	`NoProgressCooldown`, `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, `HeartbeatInterval`.
274	Migrates **all three production dispatch sites** (CLI `ddx work`,
275	server `runWorker`, GraphQL `StartWorker`) plus ~44 test sites.
276	Retires the legacy `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions` + `Run` once all
277	callers migrate. Adds behavioral e2e tests on every dispatch path
278	proving `.ddx/config.yaml` actually drives the running loop. This
279	stage blocks FEAT-022 Stage G.
280	
281	**Stage 2 — RunOptions migration.**
282	Migrates `RunOptions` → `AgentRunRuntime` using the same
283	add-alongside-then-retire pattern. ~14 constructor sites including
284	`CompareOptions` and `QuorumOptions` (which embed `RunOptions`).
285	New test-config constructor variant for the run path.
286	
287	**Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration.**
288	Migrates `ExecuteBeadOptions` → `ExecuteBeadRuntime`. ~16 sites.
289	Adds `ContextBudget` to `EvidenceCapsConfig` (currently has no
290	config home). Wires `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` (currently parsed but
291	not consumed) into the resolved-config path.
292	
293	**Stage 4 — Lint enforcement and test debt cleanup.**
294	Adds a structural lint analyzer (`runtimelint`) using a closed-list
295	of forbidden field names scoped to `*Runtime` structs in
296	`cli/internal/agent/`. Reuses the analyzer pattern from FEAT-022
297	Stage A2 so the new rule plugs into the same Lefthook + CI hooks.
298	Resolves the four disabled config tests per evidence-driven
299	decisions specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution: two are
300	deleted (their target features are dead in the current loader),
301	two are rewritten against the current API.
302	
303	## Test coverage commitment
304	
305	Stage 1's e2e tests are non-negotiable. Each must:
306	
307	1. Load a real `.ddx/config.yaml` from disk that sets a non-default
308	   value for the knob under test.
309	2. Pass through `LoadAndResolve` (no test-injected options struct).
310	3. Execute the production code path (real `Run`, not a mock).
311	4. Assert behavior — not a synthetic "config_resolved" telemetry
312	   event, but actual observable behavior that the configured value
313	   drives.
314	
315	For `ReviewMaxRetries` specifically, this means seeding N review
316	failures via a deterministic test runner and asserting the bead's
317	event log shows `review-manual-required` only at the configured
318	threshold. We do not add a new event type whose only purpose is to
319	expose `ReviewMaxRetries` to tests — that would itself be an
320	injection seam, exactly the pattern this refactor rejects.
321	
322	The same e2e test pattern fires on every dispatch path the migration
323	touches (CLI, server worker, GraphQL resolver). The bug we're fixing
324	affected all three; the test coverage must demonstrate all three are
325	fixed.
326	
327	Stage 4 commits to net-positive coverage. Disabled config tests
328	either return to the suite (against the new API), get deleted with
329	a one-line rationale, or get rewritten if the underlying feature is
330	alive but the test fixture has drifted. The test count after Stage 4
331	must equal or exceed the pre-refactor count plus the new e2e and
332	accessor tests. Per-file decisions for the four `*.disabled` files
333	are specified in TD-024 §Disabled Test Resolution.
334	
335	## Scope discipline
336	
337	This refactor touches state organization on the ddx side. It does not
338	touch:
339	
340	- `agentlib.ExecuteRequest` or `agentlib.ExecuteResponse` shape.
341	- Any code in `~/Projects/agent`.
342	- The session log format (FEAT-006, TD-006).
343	- The execution bundle layout (TD-010).
344	- The bead tracker schema (SD-004, TD-004).
345	- The model catalog (SD-014).
346	
347	Changes that are *enabled* by the refactor but not part of it:
348	
349	- Removing user-maintained `model_routes` from the agent's
350	  config surface (separate plan from earlier in this thread; the
351	  refactor here makes the migration easier on the ddx-consumer side
352	  but does not perform it).
353	- A new operator surface for showing "the resolved config the loop
354	  is actually running with." Worth doing but tracked separately.
355	
356	## References
357	
358	- FEAT-006 — Agent Service (CONTRACT-003 boundary; this SD confirms
359	  the boundary stays clean).
360	- FEAT-022 — Prompt Evidence Assembly. This SD's Stage 1 unblocks
361	  FEAT-022 Stage G.
362	- SD-019 — Multi-Project Server Topology. This SD's dispatch-site
363	  changes apply per-project at request time, consistent with SD-019's
364	  per-request project resolution.
365	- TD-024 — Technical Design counterpart specifying the concrete
366	  type definitions, accessor signatures, and migration code shapes.
367

7	# Technical Design: Config-Driven Runtime Options
8	
9	## Type Definitions
10	
11	### `config.ResolvedConfig`
12	
13	Immutable value type. All fields unexported. Consumers call methods.
14	
15	Construction is sealed: zero-value `ResolvedConfig{}` and
16	`var rcfg ResolvedConfig` are valid Go expressions, but every public
17	accessor checks a sentinel field on first read and panics with a
18	specific message naming `LoadAndResolve` if the sentinel is unset.
19	This makes a zero-value `ResolvedConfig` syntactically constructible
20	but operationally unusable.
21	
22	```go
23	package config
24	
25	// ResolvedConfig is the loop/runner/reviewer's view of merged
26	// project config plus per-invocation overrides. It is constructed
27	// only by (*Config).Resolve and is safe to share across goroutines.
28	//
29	// Sealed: every accessor calls (r ResolvedConfig).requireSealed()
30	// on entry. A zero-value ResolvedConfig fails loudly on first read.
31	type ResolvedConfig struct {
32	    // sealed is set to true by Resolve. Accessors panic if false.
33	    sealed bool
34	
35	    // unexported fields holding the merged values
36	    assignee                 string
37	    reviewMaxRetries         int
38	    noProgressCooldown       time.Duration
39	    maxNoChangesBeforeClose  int
40	    heartbeatInterval        time.Duration
41	    harness                  string
42	    model                    string
43	    provider                 string
44	    modelRef                 string
45	    profile                  string
46	    minTier                  string
47	    maxTier                  string
48	    effort                   string
49	    permissions              string
50	    timeout                  time.Duration
51	    wallClock                time.Duration
52	    contextBudget            string
53	    evidenceCaps             EvidenceCapsResolution
54	    sessionLogDir            string
55	    mirrorConfig             ExecutionsMirrorResolution
56	    resolvedLadder           map[string][]string
57	    reasoningLevels          map[string][]string
58	    armModels                map[int]string
59	    sandbox                  bool
60	    keepSandbox              bool
61	    postRun                  string
62	    noReview                 bool
63	    record                   bool
64	    replay                   bool
65	}
66	
67	// requireSealed panics if r was not produced by Resolve.
68	// Called as the first statement of every public accessor.
69	func (r ResolvedConfig) requireSealed() {
70	    if !r.sealed {
71	        panic("config: ResolvedConfig used without going through " +
72	            "(*Config).Resolve or config.LoadAndResolve. " +
73	            "Production callers must obtain a ResolvedConfig from " +
74	            "LoadAndResolve; tests must use NewTestConfigFor*.")
75	    }
76	}
77	```
78	
79	**Sealed-construction edge cases (out of scope as defenses).**
80	Three escape paths exist in Go that the sentinel does not close:
81	
82	- `reflect.New(typeof(ResolvedConfig)).Elem()` returns an unsealed
83	  zero-value. Realistic only if production code calls reflect to
84	  construct routing values, which it does not.
85	- `unsafe.Pointer` reinterpretation can flip `sealed` directly.
86	  Realistic only if production code uses `unsafe`, which it does
87	  not for routing types.
88	- `gob.Decode` / `json.Unmarshal` into a `*ResolvedConfig` cannot
89	  set the unexported `sealed` field, so a deserialized value will
90	  panic on first accessor read. This is the *correct* behavior —
91	  serialization of `ResolvedConfig` is not supported and the
92	  panic surfaces the misuse loudly. The codebase does not register
93	  `ResolvedConfig` for any of these formats.
94	
95	These escape paths are documented and accepted as out of scope.
96	The lint and the `requireSealed` panic together cover every
97	realistic in-codebase construction path; closing the reflective/
98	unsafe paths would require runtime checks expensive enough to
99	hurt the loop's hot path.
100	
101	Accessor surface (one method per durable knob, ~22 total):
102	
103	```go
104	func (r ResolvedConfig) Assignee() string
105	func (r ResolvedConfig) ReviewMaxRetries() int
106	func (r ResolvedConfig) NoProgressCooldown() time.Duration
107	func (r ResolvedConfig) MaxNoChangesBeforeClose() int
108	func (r ResolvedConfig) HeartbeatInterval() time.Duration
109	func (r ResolvedConfig) Harness() string
110	func (r ResolvedConfig) Model() string
111	func (r ResolvedConfig) Provider() string
112	func (r ResolvedConfig) ModelRef() string
113	func (r ResolvedConfig) Profile() string
114	func (r ResolvedConfig) MinTier() string
115	func (r ResolvedConfig) MaxTier() string
116	func (r ResolvedConfig) Effort() string
117	func (r ResolvedConfig) Permissions() string
118	func (r ResolvedConfig) Timeout() time.Duration
119	func (r ResolvedConfig) ContextBudget() string
120	func (r ResolvedConfig) EvidenceCaps() EvidenceCapsResolution
121	func (r ResolvedConfig) SessionLogDir() string
122	func (r ResolvedConfig) MirrorConfig() ExecutionsMirrorResolution
123	func (r ResolvedConfig) ResolvedLadder() map[string][]string
124	```
125	
126	`EvidenceCapsResolution`, `ExecutionsMirrorResolution`, and the ladder
127	map returned from `ResolvedLadder()` are themselves immutable types or
128	defensively-copied snapshots. Returning a `map[string][]string` is
129	acceptable because callers reading it does not mutate the
130	`ResolvedConfig`'s underlying state — but a defensive copy is returned
131	on each call to make accidental mutation a no-op on the source.
132	
133	### `config.CLIOverrides`
134	
135	```go
136	package config
137	
138	// CLIOverrides carries per-invocation flag values that override
139	// project config. Zero values mean "no override; use config".
140	// Boolean fields use *bool so test-side and runtime-side can
141	// distinguish "explicit false" from "not set".
142	type CLIOverrides struct {
143	    Harness     string
144	    Model       string
145	    Provider    string
146	    ModelRef    string
147	    Profile     string
148	    Effort      string
149	    Permissions string
150	    MinTier     string
151	    MaxTier     string
152	    Timeout     *time.Duration
153	    NoReview    *bool
154	    Assignee    string
155	}
156	```
157	
158	`SpecOverrides` is an alias of `CLIOverrides` used at the server
159	dispatch site to map `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` fields onto the
160	`Resolve` call. Same shape, different conceptual origin.
161	
162	### `(*Config).Resolve`
163	
164	```go
165	package config
166	
167	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering the provided
168	// overrides onto cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per
169	// field. The returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage —
170	// every map and slice held by cfg's substructs is deep-cloned via
171	// per-type Clone methods before being captured.
172	//
173	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
174	// from package defaults, sealed normally. The single exception that
175	// would warrant panicking — a misconstructed cfg from internal code
176	// — is caught at LoadAndResolve, not here. This matches the
177	// existing pre-refactor pattern where config.Load() callers
178	// commonly use `cfg, _ := Load()` and tolerate load failures.
179	func (cfg *Config) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig
180	```
181	
182	Per-type Clone methods are required, not optional, to prevent
183	shared-state bugs:
184	
185	```go
186	func (a *AgentConfig) Clone() *AgentConfig                 // copies Models, ReasoningLevels, Endpoints, Routing
187	func (r *RoutingConfig) Clone() *RoutingConfig             // copies ProfileLadders, ModelOverrides, plus other maps
188	func (e *EvidenceCapsConfig) Clone() *EvidenceCapsConfig   // copies PerHarness map AND its *EvidenceCapsOverride values
189	func (m *ExecutionsMirrorConfig) Clone() *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
190	func (w *WorkersConfig) Clone() *WorkersConfig
191	```
192	
193	Each substruct that contains a map or slice (or a pointer to
194	something containing a map or slice) gets a `Clone` method that
195	recurses appropriately. `Resolve` calls these explicitly. Generic
196	shallow `*cfg` copies are forbidden — they alias every map.
197	
198	Accessors that return collection types (e.g. `ResolvedLadder()
199	map[string][]string`) return a fresh copy on each call. The cost of
200	the per-call copy is acceptable for the resolution path; nothing in
201	the loop's hot path calls these accessors per-iteration.
202	
203	### `config.LoadAndResolve`
204	
205	```go
206	package config
207	
208	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical dispatch-site helper. It loads
209	// the project's configuration from projectRoot, then calls Resolve
210	// with overrides. Returns the sealed ResolvedConfig and any
211	// load error.
212	//
213	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable
214	// ResolvedConfig built from package defaults plus overrides — the
215	// caller decides whether to surface the error or proceed. This
216	// matches the pre-refactor pattern (pre-existing call sites use
217	// `cfg, _ := config.Load(...)` and tolerate failures). Production
218	// callers that require a valid config should check the error and
219	// abort; production callers that want best-effort behavior can
220	// proceed with the defaults-resolved value.
221	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error)
222	```
223	
224	Exactly three production sites call `LoadAndResolve`:
225	
226	- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1997` — CLI `ddx work` dispatch.
227	- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:878` — server `runWorker` dispatch.
228	- `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:77` — GraphQL
229	  `StartWorker` mutation.
230	
231	Adding a fourth dispatch site (e.g. a future REST endpoint that
232	starts a worker) requires calling `LoadAndResolve` at that site.
233	There is no other supported way to obtain a `ResolvedConfig` in
234	production code.

478	## Test config constructors
479	
480	Per the user's directive: constructors, not builders. Each constructor
481	takes an explicit options struct that names every field a test might
482	care about. Zero defaults for production-relevant knobs — tests that
483	omit a field get a sentinel value that fails fast on read.
484	
485	```go
486	package config
487	
488	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the loop reads.
489	// Tests must specify each. Zero strings and zero durations are
490	// allowed only where production also accepts them.
491	type TestLoopConfigOpts struct {
492	    Assignee                string
493	    ReviewMaxRetries        int
494	    NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
495	    MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
496	    HeartbeatInterval       time.Duration
497	    Harness                 string
498	    Model                   string
499	    Profile                 string
500	    MinTier                 string
501	    MaxTier                 string
502	    EvidenceCaps            EvidenceCapsConfig
503	}
504	
505	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d,
506	// produces a ResolvedConfig matching opts exactly. Used by tests
507	// for the execute-loop path.
508	func NewTestConfigForLoop(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) *Config
509	
510	// Sibling constructors for the other paths.
511	func NewTestConfigForRun(opts TestRunConfigOpts) *Config
512	func NewTestConfigForBead(opts TestBeadConfigOpts) *Config
513	```
514	
515	`NewTestConfigForLoop` cannot be bypassed by direct struct
516	construction in tests, because the test cannot construct a
517	`ResolvedConfig` itself (no exported fields). The only way to obtain
518	one is via `Resolve` on a `*Config`, and the only test-friendly way
519	to obtain a `*Config` is via these constructors.

521	## Lint rule (Stage 4)
522	
523	Reuses the analyzer pattern from FEAT-022 Stage A2 (the
524	`evidencelint` analyzer in `cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/`). Adds a
525	sibling analyzer `runtimelint`.
526	
527	**Scope discipline:** the lint applies *only* to struct types whose
528	name ends in `Runtime` and that are declared in `cli/internal/agent/`.
529	This avoids false-positives on result/status/event types that
530	legitimately contain fields named `Harness`, `Model`, `Provider`,
531	etc. — those are observable outputs of an invocation, not durable
532	inputs. Codex flagged this risk concretely: `cli/internal/agent/types.go`
533	has multiple result/status structs at lines 67-69 and 131-143 that
534	must not trigger the rule.
535	
536	The analyzer flags:
537	
538	1. **Forbidden field names on `*Runtime` structs.** Any struct in
539	   `cli/internal/agent/` whose name ends in `Runtime` containing a
540	   field whose name matches one of the durable-knob names from the
541	   classification table above. Concrete forbidden names (closed
542	   list, not regex-derived to avoid drift):
543	   `Harness`, `Model`, `Provider`, `ModelRef`, `Profile`, `Effort`,
544	   `Permissions`, `Timeout`, `WallClock`, `ContextBudget`, `MinTier`,
545	   `MaxTier`, `Assignee`, `ReviewMaxRetries`, `NoProgressCooldown`,
546	   `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`, `HeartbeatInterval`,
547	   `SessionLogDir` (durable variant), `MirrorCfg`, `Models`,
548	   `ReasoningLevels`, `Endpoints`, `ProfileLadders`,
549	   `ModelOverrides`, `PerHarness`.
550	   Closed list is maintained as a Go constant; adding a new durable
551	   knob to `*Config` requires adding its name to this list.
552	
553	   `NoReview`, `PollInterval`, and `SessionLogDirOverride` are
554	   explicitly NOT on the forbidden list — all three are runtime
555	   intent (per-invocation), not durable. The lint must allow them
556	   on `*Runtime` structs. Note `SessionLogDir` (durable) is
557	   forbidden but `SessionLogDirOverride` (per-invocation override)
558	   is allowed; the rename in the runtime struct enforces the
559	   distinction.
560	
561	2. **No reintroduction of legacy options types.** Composite literal
562	   `Xxx{...}` where `Xxx` is one of `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`,
563	   `RunOptions`, `ExecuteBeadOptions`, `CompareOptions`,
564	   `QuorumOptions` and the surrounding package is anywhere in the
565	   repo. These types must not exist post-cleanup; this catches
566	   reintroduction.
567	
568	3. **No `*Options` parameters.** Any function declared in
569	   `cli/internal/agent/` whose signature includes a parameter typed
570	   as one of the legacy options types named in (2). These types
571	   must not exist post-cleanup; this catches accidental restoration.
572	
573	4. **`PollInterval` exempt.** `PollInterval` is runtime intent, not
574	   durable — it's the loop's tick rate, scoped to a single
575	   invocation. The lint must not flag it. Codex explicitly flagged
576	   this as a false-positive risk on the original regex-based rule.
577	
578	The lint plugs into the same Lefthook pre-commit hook and CI job as
579	`evidencelint`. Stage 4's acceptance includes running the analyzer
580	against the post-cleanup tree and asserting zero violations.

631	
632	```
633	Stage 1 (foundation + loop)
634	    │
635	    ├──> blocks: FEAT-022 Stage G (ddx-70c1d2e2)
636	    │
637	Stage 2 (RunOptions)            ── independent of Stage 3
638	Stage 3 (ExecuteBeadOptions)    ── independent of Stage 2
639	    │
640	Stage 4 (lint + disabled tests) ── depends on Stages 1, 2, 3
641	```
642	
643	Stage 1 must land first because it introduces the foundation types
644	(`ResolvedConfig`, `Resolve`, `LoadAndResolve`, the test-config
645	constructors). Stages 2 and 3 reuse those types and are otherwise

649	## Test plan
650	
651	Each stage adds tests that go through the production code path, not
652	test-injection shortcuts. The e2e tests are behavioral, not
653	introspective: they assert **what the loop does**, not **what
654	internal state the loop holds**. This eliminates the temptation to
655	add synthetic telemetry events whose only purpose is to expose
656	internals — which would itself be the injection-seam pattern this
657	refactor rejects.
658	
659	**Stage 1 e2e behavioral tests (non-negotiable):**
660	
661	For `ReviewMaxRetries` (the bug class's load-bearing case):
662	
663	```
664	TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI:
665	  given a temp project root with .ddx/config.yaml setting
666	       review_max_retries: 5,
667	  given a deterministic test runner that returns
668	       a parseable APPROVE only after the Nth review attempt,
669	  when ddx work --once is invoked against a bead seeded to fail
670	       review on the first 4 attempts and succeed on the 5th,
671	  then the bead closes successfully on attempt 5,
672	   and no review-manual-required event is emitted on attempts 1-4.
673	
674	  Companion test:
675	  given the same setup,
676	  when the bead is seeded to fail on the first 5 attempts,
677	  then the bead emits review-manual-required on attempt 5,
678	   and the bead's status transitions to manual-required.
679	
680	TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer:
681	  given a registered project with the same config,
682	  when StartWorker is invoked via runWorker,
683	  then the same observable behavior holds.
684	
685	TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigGraphQL:
686	  given the same project served via the GraphQL endpoint,
687	  when StartWorker is invoked via the GraphQL mutation,
688	  then the same observable behavior holds.
689	```
690	
691	Each test verifies the contract through observable bead events
692	(`review-manual-required`, `review-error`, bead status transitions),
693	not through inspection of any private struct field or any synthetic
694	event added for testability.
695	
696	For `NoProgressCooldown` and `MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`: similar
697	behavioral tests once their resolvers land in Stage 1. Each has
698	observable consequences (claim reattempts, bead closure after N
699	no-change iterations) that a deterministic test runner can drive.
700	
701	**Per-stage accessor tests:** every public method on `ResolvedConfig`
702	gets a unit test asserting (a) the value comes from `*Config` when no
703	override is set, (b) the override wins when set, (c) sealed-access
704	panic fires on a zero-value `ResolvedConfig{}`. ~25 accessor unit
705	tests in Stage 1.
706	
707	**Sealed-construction tests:** explicit tests that
708	`var rcfg config.ResolvedConfig` and `config.ResolvedConfig{}` panic
709	on first accessor call with a message naming `LoadAndResolve`.
710	Without these tests, future refactoring could quietly remove the
711	sentinel check.
712	
713	**Migration sanity:** each grouping of beads that migrates an options
714	family includes a "behavioral snapshot" test captured pre-rename
715	(asserting the existing observable behavior on representative
716	inputs). The same snapshot test must pass post-rename against the
717	new `*Runtime` API. Snapshots are kept in the suite until the
718	cleanup bead retires them.
719	
720	**Stage 4 lint tests:** the `runtimelint` analyzer's own test
721	fixtures include forbidden patterns (durable field on `*Runtime`,
722	legacy `*Options` literal, legacy `*Options` parameter type) and
723	assert non-zero exit per fixture. Plus a positive test on the
724	current tree post-cleanup asserting zero violations.
725	
726	## Bead inventory
727	
728	The work splits into ~30 small beads. Each fits one execute-bead
729	worker window (target <500 LoC net change, <20 min agent time, <$10
730	spend) and leaves the tree green. Stage 1 unblocks FEAT-022 Stage G;
731	Stages 2-4 follow.
732	
733	The inventory below is the authoritative breakdown the bead tracker
734	will receive. Bead IDs are assigned at `ddx bead create` time.
735	
736	### Stage 1 — Foundation + Loop migration (unblocks FEAT-022 G)
737	
738	**Foundation:**
739	
740	1. `config: ResolvedConfig type + sealed-construction sentinel +
741	   accessor unit tests`. Adds the type, the sentinel field, the
742	   `requireSealed` panic helper, and unit tests for sealed-access
743	   panic + every accessor returning zero-value-after-seal. No
744	   production code consumes it yet.
745	2. `config: CLIOverrides struct + Resolve method + per-type Clone
746	   methods + unit tests`. Adds `(*Config).Resolve` that returns a
747	   sealed `ResolvedConfig`, plus `Clone` methods on `AgentConfig`,
748	   `RoutingConfig`, `EvidenceCapsConfig`, `ExecutionsMirrorConfig`,
749	   `WorkersConfig`. Tests assert deep-copy correctness on each
750	   clone path.
751	3. `config: LoadAndResolve helper + unit tests`. The single
752	   canonical entry point used by all dispatch sites.
753	4. `config: NoProgressCooldown / MaxNoChangesBeforeClose /
754	   HeartbeatInterval resolvers on WorkersConfig + unit tests`.
755	   Adds the three resolvers with sensible defaults matching today's
756	   hardcoded constants. Loop doesn't read them yet.
757	5. `config: NewTestConfigForLoop constructor + opts struct + unit
758	   tests`. Tests construct a `*Config` via this, call `Resolve`,
759	   and assert every loop-relevant accessor returns the configured
760	   value.
761	
762	**Loop add-alongside:**
763	
764	6. `agent: ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct (durable fields stripped)
765	   + RunWithConfig method (alongside existing Run) + unit test`.
766	   New method delegates to existing implementation under the hood;
767	   no behavior change. Depends on beads 1-4 (the foundation types
768	   plus the new resolvers, since `RunWithConfig` consumes them).
769	
770	6.5. `agent/test: deterministic review-failure runner fixture
771	   + helper for behavioral e2e tests`. Production review path has
772	   no test seam today (`grep TestReviewRetry|fakeReviewer` returns
773	   zero hits). Beads 7-9 each need this fixture to drive
774	   N-deterministic-failures-then-success behavior. Centralizing the
775	   fixture in one bead means beads 7-9 reuse it instead of each
776	   reinventing it (and disagreeing on the seam shape).
777	
778	**Loop production migration (one site per bead):**
779	
780	7. `cmd/work: migrate ddx work / execute-loop CLI dispatch to
781	   LoadAndResolve + RunWithConfig + behavioral e2e test for
782	   ReviewMaxRetries threshold`. Acceptance: behavioral test
783	   `TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigCLI` passes against a real
784	   `.ddx/config.yaml`. Depends on bead 6.5 (fixture).
785	8. `server/workers: migrate runWorker dispatch to LoadAndResolve +
786	   RunWithConfig + behavioral e2e test`. Acceptance: behavioral
787	   test `TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigServer` passes. Depends
788	   on bead 6.5.
789	9. `server/graphql: migrate StartWorker resolver to LoadAndResolve +
790	   behavioral e2e test`. Acceptance: behavioral test
791	   `TestReviewRetryThresholdFromConfigGraphQL` passes. Depends on
792	   bead 6.5.
793	10. `agent: behavioral e2e tests for NoProgressCooldown +
794	    MaxNoChangesBeforeClose`. Same shape as the
795	    `ReviewMaxRetries` triplet, against the new resolvers from
796	    bead 4.
797	
798	**Loop test migration (mechanical, grouped by file — per round-3
799	oversize splits):**
800	
801	11a. `agent/test: migrate first half of execute_bead_loop_test.go
802	    (~520 LoC, ~12 ExecuteBeadLoopOptions literals) off legacy
803	    options to RunWithConfig`.
804	11b. `agent/test: migrate second half of execute_bead_loop_test.go
805	    (~520 LoC, ~11 ExecuteBeadLoopOptions literals)`.
806	12a. `agent/test: migrate execute_bead_review_retry_test.go +
807	    execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go +
808	    execute_bead_review_taxonomy_test.go (~486 LoC)`.
809	12b. `agent/test: migrate execute_bead_review_evidence_test.go +
810	    execute_bead_review_test.go (~600+ LoC)`.
811	13. `agent/test: migrate execute_bead_integration_test.go +
812	    tier_escalation_integration_test.go`.
813	14. `agent/test: migrate cmd/agent_metrics_review_evidence_test.go
814	    + cmd/agent_execute_loop_test.go`.
815	15a. `server/test: migrate workers_test.go (~1107 LoC) — bulk
816	    mechanical rename`.
817	15b. `server/test: migrate workers_watchdog_test.go +
818	    workers_stop_propagation_test.go + any sibling
819	    workers_*_test.go file that constructs loop options`.
820	
821	**Loop cleanup:**
822	
823	16. `agent: retire ExecuteBeadLoopOptions + Run method (legacy
824	    path)`. After all callers migrated. Includes deletion of any
825	    transitional shims.
826	
827	This unblocks FEAT-022 Stage G — bead 7 is the one that surfaces
828	`ReviewMaxRetries` from `.ddx/config.yaml` to the running CLI loop.
829	Bead 8 does the same for the server worker (which is what
830	`ddx work` actually dispatches to today).
831	
832	### Stage 2 — RunOptions migration
833	
834	17. `agent: AgentRunRuntime struct + RunWithConfig (alongside Run) +
835	    NewTestConfigForRun constructor`.
836	18. `cmd/agent_run: migrate ddx agent run dispatch + behavioral
837	    test`.
838	19. `cmd/agent_compare: migrate ddx agent compare + behavioral
839	    test`.
840	20. `cmd/agent_quorum: migrate ddx agent quorum + behavioral test`.
841	21. `agent/test: migrate RunOptions test sites (5-10 sites)`.
842	22. `agent: retire RunOptions + Run legacy method`.
843	
844	### Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration
845	
846	23. `agent: ExecuteBeadRuntime struct + RunWithConfig (alongside
847	    Run) + NewTestConfigForBead constructor`.
848	24. `config: add ContextBudget to EvidenceCapsConfig + resolver +
849	    unit test`.
850	25. `cmd/agent_execute_bead: migrate ddx agent execute-bead
851	    dispatch + behavioral test`.
852	26. `agent: wire ExecutionsConfig.Mirror into ExecuteBeadRuntime
853	    via ResolvedConfig.MirrorConfig() + behavioral test`.
854	27a. `agent/test: migrate ExecuteBeadOptions sites in
855	    execute_bead_artifacts_test.go + execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go +
856	    execute_bead_env_isolation_test.go (~6 sites)`.
857	27b. `agent/test: migrate ExecuteBeadOptions sites in
858	    executions_mirror_test.go + execute_bead_routing_evidence_test.go +
859	    integration_helper_test.go + agent_runner_service_test.go (~6 sites)`.
860	27c. `agent/test: migrate remaining ExecuteBeadOptions sites in
861	    execute_bead_e2e_test.go + cmd/ test files (~4 sites)`.
862	28. `agent: retire ExecuteBeadOptions + legacy method`.
863	
864	### Stage 4 — Lint enforcement + test debt cleanup
865	
866	29. `tools/lint: runtimelint analyzer (closed-list field-name
867	    forbidden + *Options-type forbidden) + unit tests + Lefthook
868	    + CI wiring`.
869	30. `config/test: rewrite config_enhanced_test.go against current
870	    schema + Resolve validation paths`.
871	31. `config/test: write fresh library-path-resolution tests
872	    against ResolveLibraryResource; delete
873	    config_library_test.go.disabled`.
874	32. `config/test: delete config_us018_test.go.disabled +
875	    config_us019_test.go.disabled (dead-feature confirmation
876	    in commit messages)`.
877	33. `agent: final sweep — runtimelint passes against the tree`.
878	
879	### Bead dependency graph
880	
881	- Foundation beads (1-5) are independent of each other except 2
882	  depends on 1 (`Resolve` returns a `ResolvedConfig`).
883	- Loop add-alongside (6) depends on 1-4 (foundation types + the new
884	  resolvers, since `RunWithConfig` consumes them).
885	- 6.5 (review-failure fixture) depends on 6.
886	- Loop production migration (7, 8, 9) each depends on 6.5; bead 10
887	  depends on 4 (the new resolvers).
888	- Loop test migration (11a, 11b, 12a, 12b, 13, 14, 15a, 15b)
889	  depends on 6 and is otherwise parallel.
890	- Loop cleanup (16) depends on 7-10 + 11a/b + 12a/b + 13 + 14 +
891	  15a/b (every caller migrated).
892	- **Stage 2 add-alongside (17) depends only on beads 1-3** (not on
893	  bead 16). Per round-3 review: `RunOptions` is independent of
894	  `ExecuteBeadLoopOptions`, so Stage 2's add-alongside can start
895	  immediately after foundation. Only Stage 2's cleanup (22)
896	  depends on bead 16 — both retirements need to coordinate to
897	  avoid leaving orphan `*Runtime` ↔ `*Options` shims.
898	- **Stage 3 add-alongside (23) depends only on beads 1-3.** Same
899	  reasoning. Stage 3's cleanup (28) depends on bead 16.
900	- Stage 2 cleanup (22) depends on 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21.
901	- Stage 3 cleanup (28) depends on 16, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27a, 27b, 27c.
902	- Stage 4 lint (29) depends on 16, 22, 28.
903	- Stage 4 test debt (30-32) is independent.
904	- Stage 4 final sweep (33) depends on 29.
